behaviors.description: Renamed files appear in their own category and are not also counted as a deletion plus an addition.
behaviors.justification: Real failure boundary is the Git subprocess + rename detection semantics, not just local counting logic. A mock can hide failures such as invoking git without rename detection or mis-parsing real rename status formats. Rejected higher levels: behavior text is purely git/CLI reporting with no AI/model step, so Agentic/Workflow are disqualified.
behaviors.test_description: Create a real temporary git repo, commit a file, rename it (`git mv`), run the CLI against the repo, and assert report shows 1 rename and 0 add/delete for that file.

behaviors.description: Diffs that include binary files do not cause the tool to crash; binary files are counted in the output.
behaviors.justification: Binary diff handling depends on real git output and subprocess I/O behavior (including non-text diff metadata). In-process fakes can miss decode/crash paths triggered only by actual binary diff output. Rejected higher levels: behavior contains no AI/ML decision step, so Agentic/Workflow are disqualified.
behaviors.test_description: In a real temp git repo, commit a binary file change, run the CLI, assert process exits successfully and report includes the binary file in the correct category/count.

behaviors.description: Running the tool on a repository that has no commits yet produces an empty report rather than an error.
behaviors.justification: No-commit repositories have specific real git error/exit behavior (unborn HEAD) that must be exercised through actual subprocess calls. Mocking git can hide this boundary condition. Rejected higher levels: behavior is repository state handling only; no AI/model sequencing is present.
behaviors.test_description: Initialize a real git repo with no commits, run the CLI, assert successful exit and an empty structured report instead of an exception/error output.

behaviors.description: The counts in the report match the actual change history of the repository being analyzed.
behaviors.justification: This validates end-to-end correctness against real repository history and git command outputs across commits; module-only tests with stubs can pass while real history parsing fails. Rejected higher levels: issue scope is deterministic git history accounting, with no AI-driven outputs.
behaviors.test_description: Build a real git repo history containing known numbers of adds/deletes/renames/modifications across multiple commits, run the CLI, and assert report counts exactly match the known ground truth.

behaviors.description: The output is identical whether the changes being analyzed are staged or unstaged.
behaviors.justification: Staged vs unstaged behavior depends on real git index/worktree semantics and subprocess calls; fakes often miss index-state edge cases. This is a real infra boundary (CLI + git + filesystem state). Rejected higher levels: no LLM/model output quality or multi-agent sequence is involved.
behaviors.test_description: In a real temp repo, create equivalent change sets once as staged and once as unstaged, run the CLI in both states, and assert outputs are byte-for-byte identical.
